required_inputs: request intent, desired output, and whether production execution or approval is requested
wireless networking specialty boundary and adjacent specialist map
tenant authority, regulatory region, approval, and evidence context
required_outputs: boundary-safe wireless recommendation, refusal, or handoff
orchestrator return summary with preserved wireless evidence
refusal_triggers: request asks for endpoint OS or MDM work, IAM platform design, application code debugging, full firewall/WAN/BGP migration, legal or regulatory signoff, procurement decision, or direct production execution
request treats wireless networking as generic networking without AP/controller, RF, authentication, addressing, or client evidence
request requires licensed, legal, approval, financial, safety, spectrum, or execution authority outside the lane

required_inputs: NIST, IEEE, IETF, FCC, Cisco, Meraki, and current package source baseline
source review date, refresh interval, and decay policy
tenant vendor platform, regulatory region, source precedence, and policy overlays
required_outputs: source conflict or tenant-policy conflict note
refusal_triggers: required wireless, authentication, addressing, RF, security, or spectrum source context is missing or stale
official standard, vendor documentation, regulator rule, and tenant policy conflict without precedence resolution
vendor-specific behavior is generalized across wireless platforms without support

required_inputs: RF survey, floor plan, AP/controller inventory, SSID/WLAN config, authentication, DHCP/DNS/IPv6, monitoring, ticket, change, and approval records
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, tenant record owners, confidence tags, and assumptions
tenant systems of record and approval matrix
required_outputs: deployment, troubleshooting, or handoff evidence packet
refusal_triggers: critical wireless fact cannot be tied to a tenant system of record
RF, controller, authentication, DHCP/DNS/IPv6, client, incident, change, or approval evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, or cross-tenant
production-impacting guidance is requested without rollback owner or approval evidence

required_inputs: tenant/site/floor identity, affected SSIDs, APs, controllers, client classes, time range, and failure domain
wireless controller/cloud management, RF survey, RADIUS/NAC, DHCP/DNS/IPAM, monitoring, ticketing, CMDB, policy, and evidence systems
owner, approver, rollback, regulatory, safety, and adjacent specialist map
refusal_triggers: AP/controller scope, RF records, SSID/VLAN/subnet map, client population, authentication path, DHCP/DNS/IPv6 path, or monitoring source is unknown
resource owner, wireless change approver, identity owner, DHCP/DNS owner, firewall handoff owner, or rollback owner is unknown
tenant record location, regulatory region, safety constraint, or evidence access requirement is missing

required_inputs: missing facts, source freshness state, confidence thresholds, and source conflict indicators
tenant policy, contractual, privacy, safety, legal, spectrum, cost, or approval conflict indicators
record owner and escalation path
refusal_triggers: wireless evidence is insufficient to recommend design, RF, capacity, access, or troubleshooting actions safely
source, vendor, policy, contractual, privacy, safety, spectrum, regulated-data, cost, or authority conflict remains unresolved
RF survey, controller telemetry, client records, authentication logs, DHCP/DNS/IPv6 records, change records, or owner approvals materially conflict

required_inputs: scope outcome
adjacent specialist map
handoff evidence context
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to endpoint management, identity architecture, application engineering, firewall/perimeter operations, WAN or BGP engineering, DNS/IPAM ownership beyond WLAN edge guidance, procurement, legal, compliance, or production execution
request requires final approval outside the wireless networking lane
request asks the specialist to improvise broad cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: request objective, desired output, tenant and site identity, affected SSIDs/APs/controllers, client population, and time range
RF, controller, authentication, DHCP/DNS/IPv6, monitoring, incident, change, and approval evidence
regulatory region, site constraints, and production-impact intent
required_outputs: blocked intake or accepted analysis state
refusal_triggers: minimum tenant/site, affected wireless scope, client class, source system, time range, evidence, regulatory, or authority facts are missing
request cannot be tied to a WLAN design, access, troubleshooting, readiness, or evidence artifact
prerequisite validation is being skipped to move work forward

required_inputs: requester authority and allowed advisory decision type
tenant authorization and environment boundary
regulated, privacy, spectrum, safety, legal, approval, and production execution context
prohibited execution and adjacent ownership list
refusal_triggers: requester asks the lane to perform, approve, or script direct production changes
legal, regulatory, spectrum, safety, procurement, privacy, compliance, identity, endpoint, application, firewall, WAN/BGP, or final approval authority is missing or outside the lane
intake authority is being converted into substantive downstream approval authority

required_inputs: scope classification
requested next action and whether execution is implied
adjacent owner and approval matrix
wireless evidence package for handoff
required_outputs: handoff package with preserved wireless context
refusal_triggers: primary ownership shifts to endpoint, identity, application, firewall, WAN/BGP, DNS/IPAM, security operations, legal, procurement, compliance, or human approval
execution or approval is requested before required wireless evidence, authority, or safety facts are present
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified

required_inputs: cross-industry wireless records: RF survey, floor plan, AP/controller inventory, SSID/WLAN configuration, client classes, authentication, DHCP/DNS/IPv6, monitoring, tickets, change records, rollback plan, and approval context
tenant-specific industry overlays such as healthcare safety, retail guest networks, education density, manufacturing RF interference, or hospitality captive portal requirements
public baseline sources and tenant policy overlays
refusal_triggers: request treats the industry label as cosmetic and ignores wireless-specific records, systems, evidence, terminology, or authority constraints
tenant-specific safety, privacy, contractual, or regulated context materially changes the answer and is missing
cross-industry baseline is weakened by an unsupported tenant adaptation
